name: Laguna del Cañizar and Cella Spring Loop
name_original: Laguna del Cañizar y Fuente de Cella — circular por la Comarca de Teruel
description: This easy 14.9-mile mountain biking route circles the Laguna del Cañizar and Fuente de Cella, offering wetland views and historical features
region_intro: Mountain biking around Cella features a landscape characterized by gentle gradients, agricultural plains, and areas with pine forests and wetlands. The region offers a mix of converted railway paths, known as Vías Verdes, alongside trails that traverse farmland and natural lagoons. Elevations are generally moderate, providing accessible routes for various skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Cella The most popular mountain bike trail is Vía Verde Ojos Negros — circular desde Santa Eulalia del Campo, a 16.2 miles (26.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 58 minutes to complete. This route follows a historic greenway, offering a smooth and scenic ride. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Balsa del Pinar de Rubiales from El Campillo, an easy 12.3 miles (19.8 km) path. This trail leads through pine forests and offers views of the Balsa del Pinar de Rubiales. Local mountain bikers also love the Laguna del Cañizar and Cella Spring Loop, a 14.9 miles (24.1 km) trail leading through wetlands and past the Cella Spring, often completed in about 1 hour 43 minutes. Mountain biking around Cella is defined by greenways, agricultural landscapes, and natural lagoons, with options for different ability levels. The network includes easy routes suitable for families and more challenging paths with varied terrain. The routes in Cella are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 35 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cella's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails can I find around Cella?
A: Mountain biking around Cella offers a diverse landscape characterized by gentle gradients, agricultural plains, and areas with pine forests and wetlands. You'll find a mix of converted railway paths, known as Vías Verdes, alongside trails that traverse farmland and natural lagoons. Elevations are generally moderate, providing accessible routes for various skill levels.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Cella?
A: There are over 45 mountain bike trails around Cella, catering to a range of abilities. This includes 5 easy routes, 29 moderate routes, and 12 more challenging options.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly mountain bike trails in Cella?
A: Yes, Cella offers several easy routes suitable for families and beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005528814/via-verde-ojos-negros-circular-desde-santa-eulalia-del-campo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Ojos Negros — circular desde Santa Eulalia del Campo&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 16.2-mile trail that follows a historic greenway, offering a smooth and scenic ride. Another great option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004982958/balsa-del-pinar-de-rubiales-from-el-campillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsa del Pinar de Rubiales from El Campillo&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 12.3-mile path through pine forests.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Cella?
A: The mountain bike routes in Cella are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 35 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cella's varied terrain, often praising the greenways, agricultural landscapes, and natural lagoons.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Cella?
A: Yes, Cella features several excellent circular routes. A local favorite is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005543894/laguna-del-canizar-and-cella-spring-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Cañizar and Cella Spring Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 14.9-mile trail that takes you through wetlands and past the Cella Spring. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005528814/via-verde-ojos-negros-circular-desde-santa-eulalia-del-campo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Ojos Negros — circular desde Santa Eulalia del Campo&lt;/a&gt; is also a popular circular option.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: While riding, you can explore various natural monuments and historical sites. Highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Clays of Teruel&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/756481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Bezas&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arquillo de San Blas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4547706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Shelter of the Archer of the Enclosed Ravines&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/689732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabrerizo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offering scenic views.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for more experienced riders seeking a challenge, Cella offers 12 difficult routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19814035" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path Through Farmland – Peracense Castle loop from Santa Eulalia del Campo&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 44.6-mile route with significant elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Cella?
A: Given the region's agricultural plains and wetlands, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Cella, with milder temperatures and lush landscapes. Summer can be warm, while winter might bring colder weather, though many trails remain accessible.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the mountain bike trails in Cella?
A: While specific regulations can vary by trail section or protected area, many natural trails in Cella are generally dog-friendly, especially if your dog is well-behaved and kept on a leash. Always be mindful of local signage and other trail users, particularly in agricultural areas or near wildlife.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails in Cella?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For routes starting in or near towns like Santa Eulalia del Campo or El Campillo, you can often find public parking areas. For more remote trailheads, look for designated parking spots or safe roadside pull-offs, always ensuring you do not obstruct access or private property.

Q: Can I access mountain bike trails in Cella using public transport?
A: Public transport options directly to trailheads might be limited in some of the more rural areas around Cella. However, main towns in the region may have bus connections. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for routes that pass near your desired starting points, keeping in mind that bikes might have restrictions on board.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: This circular mountain bike route is approximately 24 kilometers long. With an easy difficulty rating and minimal elevation gain, it typically takes around 1 hour and 43 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is rated as easy, making it suitable for beginners and those looking for a relaxed ride. The terrain is generally flat with very little elevation gain, characteristic of wetland areas.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Laguna del Cañizar y Fuente de Cella mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact route aren't detailed, you can typically find parking in or near the towns of Cella or Villarquemado, which are close to the Laguna del Cañizar and Cella Spring. Look for designated public parking spots in these areas.

faqs.question: Is the Laguna del Cañizar y Fuente de Cella route a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, as indicated by its name, this is a circular route. You will start and end at the same point, offering a continuous and varied experience without needing to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for mountain biking on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this route is generally easy, characterized by flat sections, especially around the wetlands. You can expect a mix of forest roads, double tracks, and potentially some unpaved or gravel sections. It's not technically challenging, making it enjoyable for a wide range of riders.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route highlights two significant natural features: the Laguna del Cañizar, a large inland wetland known for its biodiversity and birdwatching opportunities, and the Cella Spring, an interesting point associated with historical engineering. The surrounding Comarca de Teruel also offers picturesque natural scenery.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike this route is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural landscape is vibrant. Summer can be hot, while winter might bring colder temperatures, though the low elevation makes it accessible for much of the year.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other official trails, including the Camino Natural Santander Mediterráneo. Tramo CN VV Ojos Negros: Santa Eulalia - Puerto Escandón, the Vía Verde Ojos Negros, and the GR 160 - Camino del Cid BTT, among others. This means you might encounter other cyclists or hikers on parts of the path.

faqs.question: Is the Laguna del Cañizar y Fuente de Cella route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in natural areas like this, but it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially around the Laguna del Cañizar due to its ecological importance and presence of wildlife. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: No, typically you do not need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to mountain bike this specific route. Access to the natural areas like the Laguna del Cañizar and Cella Spring is generally free for recreational use.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the route for a post-ride meal?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is located within the Comarca de Teruel, and the nearby towns of Cella and Villarquemado offer various amenities. You can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in these towns to refuel and relax after your ride.
